_meta.description: Three balanced applicant sets for the scholarship-selection agentic-AI control experiment. Each set has 8 applicants; participants select 3. Sets are matched on difficulty: each contains the same archetype distribution so no set is easier than another.

scale_notes.incomeDecile: 1 = lowest income (most need), 10 = highest income (least need)
scale_notes.archetype: internal balancing label, NOT shown to participants

setA.name: Ethan Caldwell
setA.essaySummary: Aspiring researcher with strong academic record; cites family support and ample resources.
setA.flag: Top grades but limited financial need.

setA.name: Maya Fernandes
setA.essaySummary: First-generation student balancing part-time work and a heavy course load; consistent excellence.

setA.name: Dylan Brooks
setA.essaySummary: Faced housing instability; grades dipped during a difficult year but trajectory is improving.

setA.name: Priya Nair
setA.essaySummary: Runs a community tutoring program for younger students in her neighborhood.

setA.name: Jonah Reyes
setA.essaySummary: Solid mid-range profile; no single standout dimension.

setA.name: Sofia Lindqvist
setA.essaySummary: Volunteer EMT and choir organizer; deeply engaged in civic life.

setA.name: Marcus Webb
setA.essaySummary: Compelling personal narrative about overcoming a learning disability; metrics understate him.

setA.name: Hana Kim
setA.essaySummary: Reliable, well-rounded applicant without a distinguishing feature.

setB.essaySummary: National science-fair finalist from a well-resourced school.

setB.name: Devon Clarke

setB.name: Grace Yamamoto
setB.essaySummary: Dependable, well-rounded applicant without a distinguishing feature.

setC.essaySummary: Commutes two hours daily, works weekends, and still leads the debate team.
